compiled_state.title: Create UserPromptSubmit hook script, register in plugin.json, and document spike results
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

### Research step (do FIRST)
Before writing any code, verify the actual UserPromptSubmit payload field name:
1. Check Claude Code docs or source for the UserPromptSubmit hook input schema
2. If undiscoverable: create a minimal hook that dumps raw stdin to a temp log file, register it, and inspect the log after sending a test message
3. Update the hook script and tests to use the correct field name

### Implementation
1. Create plugins/dso/hooks/fix-bug-skill-directive.sh:
   - Read JSON from stdin (same pattern as other dispatchers: INPUT=$(cat))
   - Extract user message using parse_json_field from hooks/lib/deps.sh
   - Check if message matches /fix-bug or /dso:fix-bug pattern (slash-anchored — must have leading /)
   - If matched: output directive to stdout instructing agent to invoke the Skill tool
   - If not matched: output nothing
   - Always exit 0
2. Register in plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json:
   - Add UserPromptSubmit section to hooks object
   - Matcher: '' (empty — fires on all messages; script does filtering)
   - Command: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.sh fix-bug-skill-directive.sh
3. Document spike results as ticket comment to bf19-4327:
   - Explicitly note: unit tests verify script behavior only, NOT Claude Code dispatch reliability
   - E2E validation method: describe how you manually verified the hook fires in Claude Code (what session logs or context injection you checked)
   - Pass/fail determination for the spike question (does UserPromptSubmit fire reliably from plugin.json?)
   - Any workarounds needed (e.g., if plugin-level registration is unreliable, note fallback to settings.local.json)
   - Recommendation for story 65ad-b88a

### TDD
Task 1's tests go from RED → GREEN after creating the script.

### TDD exemption for plugin.json
Unit exemption criterion 1 — no conditional logic, purely structural hook registration wiring.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bash tests/run-all.sh` passes (exit 0) — all tests GREEN
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] Hook script exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x plugins/dso/hooks/fix-bug-skill-directive.sh
- [ ] plugin.json contains UserPromptSubmit section
  Verify: grep -q 'UserPromptSubmit' plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json
- [ ] Hook outputs directive when /fix-bug is in input
  Verify: echo '{"user_message":"/fix-bug crash"}' | bash plugins/dso/hooks/fix-bug-skill-directive.sh | grep -q 'Skill'
- [ ] Hook outputs nothing when /fix-bug is NOT in input
  Verify: test -z "$(echo '{"user_message":"fix the login"}' | bash plugins/dso/hooks/fix-bug-skill-directive.sh)"
- [ ] Spike results documented as ticket comment with explicit E2E validation method
  Verify: .claude/scripts/dso ticket show bf19-4327 | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); comments=[c['body'] for c in d.get('comments',[])]; found=any('E2E' in c or 'spike' in c.lower() for c in comments); sys.exit(0 if found else 1)"
- [ ] No RED markers remain in .test-index
  Verify: ! grep -q 'fix-bug-skill-directive.*\[' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Gap Analysis Amendment

### Rollback plan (if spike proves unreliable)
If UserPromptSubmit does NOT fire reliably:
1. Revert the plugin.json registration (remove UserPromptSubmit section)
2. Keep the hook script and test file for future reference (they validate the script logic)
3. Document the failure mode and workaround (settings.local.json fallback) in the spike results comment
4. Create a follow-up task on story 65ad-b88a noting that plugin-level registration failed and client-level registration is required

### Registration schema verification
Before editing plugin.json, verify that UserPromptSubmit supports the same registration schema as other hook types (matcher field, command field). If it differs, adapt the registration accordingly.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 5/6: Tests GREEN — all 6 tests passed (12 assertions). Ruff clean. All AC verification commands pass ✓
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: Created fix-bug-skill-directive.sh hook, registered UserPromptSubmit in plugin.json, spike results documented on bf19-4327.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
